channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Wizards of the Coast Releases Revised Dungeons & Dragons Villainous Unearthed Arcana
item.content:encoded:
Wizards of the Coast has released new revised Dungeons & Dragons villainous Unearthed Arcana. After taking into account player feedback, the design team has done some slight changes to several character subclasses in the playtest material, giving players a new way to be evil.

As with prior playtest material, the revised Dungeons & Dragons villainous Unearthed Arcana is available on D&D Beyond. In addition to the new material, Wizards of the Coast released a post of designer insight, where the developers spoke about the changes they made from the original material. The revised material spans the three villainous subclasses introduced in the Villainous Options 1 Unearthed Arcana.

First is the Circle of Titans Druid, the subclass that allows defenders of nature to go full kaiju. Overall, the changes are minor but meaningful. Titan Form now has higher AC and more Temporary HP. Enlarge/Reduce and Fire Shield have replaced Cure Wounds and Stoneskin in prepared spells. Lastly, the acid damage from Monstrous Appetite has been bumped up to d12s, greatly improving the damage.

The second subclass, the Hell Knight Fighter got more extensive changes. Their Hellforged Weapons can now deal Fire, Cold, or Necrotic Damage. The Infernal Wounds feature has been changed to de-emphasize luck. Now, Wound effects will happen every time you roll the Infernal Wound die (a d6), but rolling a 6 will activate a more potent effect. At level 7, that effect even happens on a 1.

Furthermore, the Hell Knight has received some late-game tweaks. The level 18 feature, Hellish Condemnation, is now gone, and Infernal Bargain has been revised.

Lastly, the revised Dungeons & Dragons villainous Unearthed Arcana provides some polish to the Demonic Sorcery Sorcerer. The Demonic Spell list has been revised, Abyssal Rupture has been changed from an Emanation effect to an Area of Effect. Abyssal Aura has been changed to Abyssal Realm and its effects are chosen by the player instead of determined by a roll on a chart. Lastly, the late game features have received utility and damage buffs.

In addition to this new material, Wizards of the Coast is responding to player feedback regarding transparency on how Unearthed Arcana material is being handled by the design team. This is vital since playtest material either hints at future supplements, like the return to Planescape, or are just dropped with no official reason like the Chirurgy Wizard or the Mystic.

To that end, the design team has provided some clarity to the fate of both the above mentioned subclasses and the other four villainous subclasses released before. The Circle of Titan Druid, Warrior of Venom Monk, and Path of Lament Barbarian were received very positively by D&D players, meaning there's a chance revised versions of these classes will appear in a future book.

Alternatively, the Primordial Patron Warlock had a mixed reception. Thematically, it seemed too close to the Genie Patron from the 5e supplement Tasha's Cauldron of Everything, and its key gimmick, the Elemental Node, was divisive. The team has decided that they will not be moving forward with this subclass.

FInally, the design team did confirm that feedback on the new Villainous Path Feats were very popular. The Lich and Death Knight may appear in a future supplement, with some adjustments to make the latter work for more martial classes.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/wizards-of-coast-releases-revised-dungeons-dragons-villainous-unearthed-arcana
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
